memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:27:29.snippet: Confirmed Status: • light -> REM -> deep (each sweep) • deep phase writes to MEMORY.md • DREAMS.md for human-readable summaries

memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:36:36.snippet: Key Learning: The `dreaming.enabled: true` in config is a **default**, but actual enablement requires:

memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:37:39.snippet: Key Learning: `/dreaming on` — Activates cron job; `/dreaming off` — Deactivates; `/dreaming status` — Check current state

memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:41:41.snippet: Key Learning: This is similar to how `session.reset` config sets defaults, but actual resets happen via commands.

memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:46:49.snippet: Issue Found: Config was set correctly in `openclaw.json`; `/dreaming on` enabled status to "on"; But **no cron job was actually created** in `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`; This is a **bug in OpenClaw 2026.4.5** — the slash command updates in-memory status but doesn't persist the c

memory:memory/2026-04-07.md:58:60.snippet: CPU Regression: **Issue #62051**: v2026.4.5 causes worker processes to load all plugins, CPU saturation; **Victor did NOT observe this** — system running normally; Only the dreaming cron job registration bug affects us

memory:memory/2026-04-06.md:25:25.snippet: What is Dreaming?: Background memory consolidation system that moves strong short-term signals into durable long-term memory. Runs automatically via scheduled sweeps.

memory:memory/2026-04-04.md:4:4.snippet: Summary: Production GHCR deployment working. Local dev environment fixed. Redis/solid_cache migration completed. n8n configured with single-port proxy. Key insight: memory grows from both what you save AND what you ask (bidirectional growth concept documented).

memory:memory/2026-04-04.md:23:25.snippet: Local Dev Environment Fixes ✅: Added build args `USER_ID` and `GROUP_ID` in docker-compose.yml; Pre-create `node_modules` directory with correct ownership; **Result**: Local dev `docker compose up` works seamlessly

memory:memory/2026-04-04.md:28:31.snippet: Build Pipeline Understanding: **Two independent builds**:; **Local dev**: `Dockerfile.dev` → builds on your machine; **Production**: `Dockerfile` → built by GitHub Actions, pushed to GHCR; **Production workflow**: `git push` → Actions builds → `docker compose pull` on VPS
